Arithmetic.name: abs
Arithmetic.description: Returns the absolute value of a number, removing any negative sign.

Arithmetic.name: add
Arithmetic.description: Adds two or more inputs element wise. Set filter=true to treat NaNs as 0 before summing.

Arithmetic.name: densify
Arithmetic.description: Converts a grouping field of many buckets into lesser number of only available buckets so as to make working with grouping fields computationally efficient.

Arithmetic.name: log
Arithmetic.description: Calculates the natural logarithm of the input value. Commonly used to transform data that has positive values.

Arithmetic.name: max
Arithmetic.description: Maximum value of all inputs. At least 2 inputs are required.

Arithmetic.name: min
Arithmetic.description: Minimum value of all inputs. At least 2 inputs are required.

Arithmetic.name: multiply
Arithmetic.description: Multiplies two or more inputs element wise. Set filter=true to treat NaNs as 0 before multiplication.

Arithmetic.name: sign
Arithmetic.description: Returns the sign of a number: +1 for positive, -1 for negative, and 0 for zero. If the input is NaN, returns NaN.

Arithmetic.name: signed_power
Arithmetic.description: x raised to the power of y such that final result preserves sign of x.

Arithmetic.name: sqrt
Arithmetic.description: Returns the non negative square root of x. Equivalent to power(x, 0.5); for signed roots use signed_power(x, 0.5).

Arithmetic.name: subtract
Arithmetic.description: Subtracts inputs left to right. Supports two or more inputs. Set filter=true to treat NaNs as 0 before subtraction.

Logical.name: and
Logical.description: Returns 1 if both inputs are true. Otherwise, returns 0.

Logical.name: if_else
Logical.description: Returns one of two values based on a condition.

Logical.name: less_than
Logical.description: Returns 1 if input1 is smaller than input2. Otherwise, returns 0.

Logical.name: equal
Logical.description: Returns 1 if input1 and input2 are the same. Otherwise, returns 0.

Logical.name: greater_than
Logical.description: Returns 1 if input1 is larger than input2. Otherwise, returns 0.

Logical.name: greater_or_equal
Logical.description: Returns 1 if input1 is larger or the same as input2. Otherwise, returns 0.

Logical.name: not_equal
Logical.description: Returns 1 if input1 and input2 are different. Otherwise, returns 0.

Logical.name: is_nan
Logical.description: Returns 1 when input is NaN, else returns 0.

Logical.name: not
Logical.description: Returns the logical negation of x.

Logical.name: or
Logical.description: Returns 1 if either input is true, otherwise it returns 0.

Time Series.name: days_from_last_change
Time Series.description: Calculates the number of days since the last change in the value of a given variable.

Time Series.name: hump
Time Series.description: Limits amount and magnitude of changes in input, thus reducing turnover.

Time Series.name: kth_element
Time Series.description: Returns the K-th value from a time series by looking back over a specified number of d days, with the option to ignore certain values. Commonly used for backfilling missing data.

Time Series.name: last_diff_value
Time Series.description: Returns the most recent value of x from the past d days that is different from the current value of x.

Time Series.name: ts_arg_max
Time Series.description: Returns the number of days since the maximum value occurred in the last d days of a time series.

Time Series.name: ts_arg_min
Time Series.description: Returns the number of days since the minimum value occurred in the last d days of a time series.

Time Series.name: ts_av_diff
Time Series.description: Returns x - ts_mean(x, d) with NaNs ignored.

Time Series.name: ts_backfill
Time Series.description: Replaces missing values in a time series with the most recent valid value from a lookback window.

Time Series.name: ts_corr
Time Series.description: Calculates the Pearson correlation between two variables over the past d days.

Time Series.name: ts_count_nans
Time Series.description: Counts the number of missing values in a data series over a specified number of days.

Time Series.name: ts_covariance
Time Series.description: Calculates the covariance between two time-series variables over the past d days.

Time Series.name: ts_decay_linear
Time Series.description: Applies a linear decay to time-series data over a set number of days.

Time Series.name: ts_delay
Time Series.description: Returns the value of a variable x from d days ago.

Time Series.name: ts_delta
Time Series.description: Calculates the difference between a value and its delayed version over a specified period.

Time Series.name: ts_mean
Time Series.description: Calculates the simple average value of a variable x over the past d days.

Time Series.name: ts_product
Time Series.description: Returns the product of the values of x over the past d days.

Time Series.name: ts_quantile
Time Series.description: Transforms ts_rank using the inverse cumulative distribution function of a chosen distribution.

Time Series.name: ts_rank
Time Series.description: Ranks the current value within a rolling time window.

Time Series.name: ts_regression
Time Series.description: Returns various parameters related to regression function.

Time Series.name: ts_scale
Time Series.description: Scales a time series to a 0-1 range based on its minimum and maximum values over a specified period.

Time Series.name: ts_std_dev
Time Series.description: Calculates the standard deviation of a data series x over the past d days.

Time Series.name: ts_step
Time Series.description: Returns a counter of days, incrementing by one each day.

Time Series.name: ts_sum
Time Series.description: Sums values of x for the past d days.

Time Series.name: ts_zscore
Time Series.description: Calculates the Z-score of a time series over a rolling window.

Cross Sectional.name: normalize
Cross Sectional.description: Centers a daily cross section by subtracting the market mean; optionally divides by the cross sectional standard deviation and clamps the result.

Cross Sectional.name: quantile
Cross Sectional.description: Ranks and shifts a vector of alpha values, then applies a chosen statistical distribution to reduce outliers.

Cross Sectional.name: rank
Cross Sectional.description: Ranks the values of the input among all instruments, returning numbers between 0.0 and 1.0.

Cross Sectional.name: scale
Cross Sectional.description: Scales the input so that the sum of absolute values across all instruments equals a specified book size.

Cross Sectional.name: winsorize
Cross Sectional.description: Limits values to within a specified number of standard deviations from the mean, reducing outliers.

Cross Sectional.name: zscore
Cross Sectional.description: Describes a value's relationship to the mean of a group of values in terms of standard deviations.

Vector.name: vec_avg
Vector.description: Calculates the mean of all elements in a vector field, converting vector data to a single matrix value.

Vector.name: vec_sum
Vector.description: Calculates the sum of all values in a vector field.

Transformational.name: bucket
Transformational.description: Creates custom groups by dividing data into buckets based on ranked values of any data field.

Transformational.name: trade_when
Transformational.description: Changes alpha values only when a specific condition is met, keeps previous values otherwise, and can close positions by assigning NaN under an exit condition.

Group.name: group_backfill
Group.description: Fills missing values for instruments within the same group using a winsorized mean over the past d days.

Group.name: group_mean
Group.description: Calculates the harmonic mean of a data field within each specified group.

Group.name: group_neutralize
Group.description: Neutralizes alpha values within each specified group by subtracting the group mean from each value.

Group.name: group_rank
Group.description: Ranks each element within its group based on the input field.

Group.name: group_scale
Group.description: Normalizes values within each group to a range between 0 and 1.

Group.name: group_zscore
Group.description: Calculates the Z-score of each value within its group.
